specific_location: The bird was seen adjacent to a creek along CR-5, SW of Ridway, CO
description: My initial feeling regarding shape was that of a large Flycatcher, with a short tail compared to Myiarchus flycatchers. The bird had an entirely dark head and auriculars, contrasting with a clean white throat. The breast had a distinct "vest" pattern with dark sides and a white center. The belly was also clean white. Undertail coverts were dark, with short tail projection past the coverts.Behaviors: The bird was seen perched on an aspen limb, singing. It remained at that perch for the duration of observation.Call: The song was composed of three parts. First was a short chip, or whip type note. The second and third parts were longer ascending then descending whistles. This matched the classic "quick three beers" call I have heard from other OSFLs.Plumage: adult
